metadata.title: Why the prior-auth AI that structurally cannot say "no" is the correct design
metadata.topic: Asymmetric decision-space design in clinical gatekeeping
metadata.purpose: Error consequences are asymmetric. A false approve is a financial overpayment, correctable on audit. A false deny withholds necessary care and is not correctable if the member's condition deteriorates. PEND is the designed escape valve routing every denial to human judgment.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: prior-auth workflow, coverage determination, AI decision liability
metadata.exclusions: LCD/NCD lookup mechanics, notification-letter format, appeal process
metadata.visual_object: A decision tree with three terminals — APPROVE (AI-reachable), PEND (AI → human → APPROVE/DENY), DENY (human-only, unreachable by AI) — annotated with volume.
metadata.hook: The skill recommends APPROVE or PEND — never DENY. That restriction is not a limitation; it is the point.

beats.narration_text: A clear orthopedic request → APPROVE. An ambiguous oncology drug → PEND, not DENY. Human reviewers own the PEND queue and make every denial. No automated denial ever closes a case against a member.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: An AI that can approve but structurally cannot deny seems half-built — why deliberately remove the deny power?
The skill recommends APPROVE or PEND — never DENY. That restriction is not a limitation; it is the point.

Error consequences are asymmetric. A false approve is a financial overpayment, correctable on audit. A false deny withholds necessary care and is not correctable if the member's condition deteriorates. PEND is the designed escape valve routing every denial to human judgment.
beats.visual: A decision tree with three terminals — APPROVE (AI-reachable), PEND (AI → human → APPROVE/DENY), DENY (human-only, unreachable by AI) — annotated with volume.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: 1,000 requests/month. AI: 700 APPROVE, 300 PEND. Humans review 300: 200 approve, 100 deny. Clinicians touch 30% of volume but make 100% of denials. Zero automated denials.

Error consequences are asymmetric. A false approve is a financial overpayment, correctable on audit. A false deny withholds necessary care and is not correctable if the member's condition deteriorates. PEND is the designed escape valve routing every denial to human judgment.
